title: SCHB ETF Review: Schwab US Broad Market (2026)
description: Complete review of SCHB ETF. Discover its 0.03% expense ratio, top holdings, performance vs VTI, and if it's the best total market fund for Schwab users.

schemas.headline: SCHB ETF Review: Schwab US Broad Market (2026)
schemas.description: Complete review of SCHB ETF. Discover its 0.03% expense ratio, top holdings, performance vs VTI, and if it's the best total market fund for Schwab users.

acceptedAnswer.text: The Schwab US Broad Market ETF (SCHB) is a low-cost exchange-traded fund that tracks the Dow Jones U.S. Broad Stock Market Index, providing exposure to large, mid, and small-cap US equities.

mainEntity.name: What is the expense ratio of SCHB in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The expense ratio for SCHB is 0.03%, making it one of the most cost-effective broad market ETFs available to investors, tied with competitors like VTI.

mainEntity.name: How does SCHB compare to VTI?

acceptedAnswer.text: SCHB and VTI are very similar, both offering broad US market exposure with identical 0.03% expense ratios. The main difference is the index tracked (Dow Jones for SCHB, CRSP for VTI), resulting in minor holdings variations, but long-term performance is nearly identical.
For investors seeking diversified, low-cost exposure to the entire US stock market, SCHB is an excellent core portfolio holding, particularly for those using Charles Schwab as their broker.
Yes, SCHB pays quarterly dividends. The yield fluctuates based on market conditions but generally aligns with the broader US stock market average.
